Ramroooo chha space .

Maak Ara is such a nice place! I absolutely loved the space, ambience, and decor—it has a really warm and aesthetic vibe. The staff are very sweet and accommodating as well.

The food portion sizes are really good for the price, which made the experience even better. I wanted something extra spicy, and they kindly served extra chili oil on the side, which I really appreciated.

Overall, a lovely place with great food, good portions, and amazing ambience!

Tried out Maak Ara recently and really loved the ambience of the place.
The drinks were refreshing and definitely the highlight for me.
Food was average, but the overall vibe and setup made the visit enjoyable.
Nice place to chill, hang out, and spend a relaxed evening with friends.

Visited with friends for breakfast, intending to stay through late lunch. The breakfast and the drinks were great. The ambiance is lovely, very airy, spacious, and aesthetically pleasing.

Unfortunately, the experience took a sharp turn because the restaurant was uncomfortably cold. With high ceilings and winter temperatures, the entire place felt freezing. We requested the staff to turn on the heater, which was clearly available for customer use and placed right next to the table we were seated at. We had chosen that table for this exact reason, as the restaurant felt quite chilly the moment we walked in. However, we were told that it was not allowed and that heaters are only turned on in the evenings. This strict heater policy was honestly baffling and felt unnecessarily rigid.

The place may feel warmer during the day, but for a restaurant that is recommended for breakfast, the morning temperature should be far more welcoming. Instead of adjusting the environment for customers, we were asked to move to a “warmer” area, which we did, but it made no difference.

Because of the cold, we finished our breakfast and rushed to find another place where we could actually sit comfortably and continue with our lunch.

Probably a great spot during hot summers, but not a comfortable choice in winter mornings.

One of my favorite spots in the Baneshwor area— perfect for hanging out with your friends. Loved the drinks, great coffee, amazing food( carbonara is my favorite), and a spacious place with gorgeous interiors.

The food quality was not satisfactory, as both the vegetables and curry seemed stale. Additionally, the staff behavior was unprofessional. The kitchen staff were extremely rude while interacting with the front-of-house staff.

Didnt find the place appealing at all.
It was crowded and dirty.
The local chicken was not promising.
The veggies were basic.(ghiralua)
When I asked for an extra serving of the tomato pickel, they outrightly denied it.

The servers were disinterested in their work.

The dish washing area was horrific and an absolute mess.

I had heard about this place several times from my colleagues. On Saturday, March 29, 2025, I had a chance to have lunch here.
There is NO parking. You have to manage your vehicle on the street.
There was a crowded of people, some people had to wait for a seat.
Food was typical Nepali taste. I liked it.
I suggest the owner to maintain more clean.

Baithak Sekuwa is a great spot if you’re craving flavorful sekuwa in a lively, aesthetic setting. The atmosphere really stands out — warm lighting, hanging plants, and the open rooftop vibe create a relaxed yet energetic environment. It’s perfect for evening hangouts with friends or casual dinner plans.

The highlight, of course, is the sekuwa. It’s well-marinated, juicy, and grilled just right with that smoky flavor that makes it truly satisfying. You can taste the freshness and proper seasoning in every bite. Their cocktails are also impressive — well-balanced, refreshing, and a great pairing with the grilled items.

Overall, Baithak Sekuwa offers a solid experience with good food and a vibrant ambiance. Definitely worth visiting if you enjoy good sekuwa and chilled evening vibes.

Baithak Sekuwa is a perfect place for authentic Nepali sekuwa lovers! 🔥 The meat was juicy, well-marinated, and grilled to perfection with that smoky flavor we all love. The ambience gives a traditional baithak vibe, making it a great spot to chill with friends and family. Service was quick and staff were friendly. Highly recommended for anyone craving real sekuwa taste!
Best food in the town and freindly staffs , greeny environment overall best best ❤️
Everything was fine expect waiter rude behvauour!!

we ordered 3 bowls of pork thukpa, 1 plate chicken steam momo and 1 plate alu chop, Don't know about momo and alu chop, because it was already too late.

The pork in pork thukpa we orders was not cleaned properly and  the food was not that warm. The pork meat in the thukpa still had the hair from the pork's skin.

When we asked to talk to the cook the manager stated that cook cannot come and talk.

The food was good, and we didn’t face any issues or negative experiences. A quiet spot if you're looking for a peaceful celebration.

I didn't have much expectation from them. Only went because my friend saw a yum looking Thukpa. But that was the least tasty among the small sets we ordered. Chicken Sekuwa was better than Thukpa. Best was the Matka Cooler. Overall unexpectedly good food with an ok enough ambience.

I had a wonderful experience at this restaurant. The hospitality was excellent from the moment I arrived, making me feel genuinely welcomed. The ambience was warm and inviting, creating the perfect setting to relax and enjoy a meal.
The service was truly outstanding, especially thanks to our server, Bhuwan, who was attentive, friendly, and made sure everything was just right throughout the visit. The food was delicious, with every dish meeting high expectations in both taste and presentation.
On top of that, the pricing was very reasonable for the quality offered. Overall, a fantastic dining experience that I would highly recommend.

Will be back soon

Recently visited this Thakali restaurant at pepsicola and had a wonderful experience. The food was excellent, authentic, and full of flavor. What impressed me most was the amazing gesture and friendliness of the staff. Their hospitality made the experience even better.

I recently had lunch at this place and tried their Chicken Thakali set. The taste was fairly average neither particularly impressive nor disappointing—but what stood out was the generous variety of accompaniments. The meal included an assortment of pickles, flavourful curries, and even a dessert, which added a nice touch to the overall experience. In terms of convenience, the location offers decent parking space for both four-wheelers and two-wheelers, which is always a plus. The ambience was pleasant and comfortable, making it a nice spot to sit and enjoy a meal. However, the highlight of my visit was undoubtedly their service. The staff were attentive and courteous, which enhanced the overall dining experience. If you happen to be in the area, this place is worth trying at least once.

However, due to its local vibe and affordable appeal, those looking for photogenic/insta worthy type place might not find this suitable. But those looking for a local hangout and superb food will surely love this place 😍.
Highly recommend the pork khaja set.
Go for dine in or order a take out, you won't be disappointed as they cater both the same with same amount and quantity.

If you know, you know! Magar Dai Ko Cafe serves some of the most authentic pork sekuwa you will find in the city.
The Food: The sekuwa is smoky, tender, and seasoned with local spices that hit all the right notes.
The Vibe: It has a very welcoming, local atmosphere that makes it great for a quick lunch or an evening hangout with friends.
Pro-Tip: It can get quite busy during peak hours because of its popularity, so try to get there a little early. Definitely the gold standard for sekuwa in the Koteshwor/Tinkune area!
Foods okay but its not sekuwa its fried in oil also the prices are very cheap compared to Portion size they provide.

This restaurant boasts a variety of dishes, with momo, burgers, and sandwiches as their signature offerings. The amazing ambiance features a bar, garden, and spacious halls. Their drinks menu, including alcoholic beverages, juice, and coffee, is equally impressive.

This place has got lot of spaces with multiple halls and outdoor tables as well. This place is owned by Yala Durbar. The food is good and the service is also fast. We went for dinner and ordered fish and chips, Hawaiian pizza and crispy chicken. The pizza was good with classic Hawaiian taste. However, the fish and chips was not proper but something like fish finger. The crispy chicken was ok. Nice place and good food. The location is also easy to get to this place.
Good ambience to stay and very friendly people serving delicious food. Nice space for kids too. Its always nice experience till now. ❤

review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Absolutely loved my experience here! The coffee,mocktails and cocktails were crafted to perfection—balanced flavors, fresh ingredients, and beautiful presentation. Each drink felt unique and thoughtfully made. The ambiance was just as impressive: cozy yet vibrant, with the perfect blend of music, lighting, and décor to set the mood. Whether you’re here for a night out or a relaxed evening, this place nails it. Highly recommend!

address: M8WV+R4F, Ghumti Kumari Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
We came here expecting decent hospitality, but the experience was extremely disappointing. It felt like they only care about their old or regular guests — if you’re new, they make you feel like you don’t matter.
The service was terrible, nobody attended to us properly, and we were never made to feel welcome. Hospitality is a business built on respect and customer service, but this place failed to provide even the basic level of both.
What made it worse was that even one of their old guests apologized to us and said, “Sorry brother, you need to leave.” That alone says a lot about how poorly the situation was handled.
Definitely not an experience I would recommend.

The pork sekuwa we ate was abismal. The meat was not fresh and their was foul smell. Absolute worst experience. I would not recommend any to visit here for a good sekuwa experience.

The food was average, but the service was really disappointing.Staff were more focused on scrolling social media rather than attending what customer asked for. Personally, I definitely wouldn't recommend this place if you're looking for good service.

Visited Sheel Sekuwa recently to try their grilled meat. The place was peaceful with fewer crowds, which I really appreciated. The staff were friendly and welcoming, and service was smooth. As for the sekuwa, I personally felt it could use a bit more masala and flavor — it just didn’t match my taste preference. But every place has its own unique style, and I’m sure many people might enjoy their lighter seasoning. Wishing them the best, and hoping to try more items from their menu next time.

This restaurant truly stands out for its amazing ambiance and exceptional food. From the moment you walk in, the atmosphere feels warm, stylish, and inviting — perfect for any occasion. The staff are friendly and attentive, making you feel well taken care of throughout the meal. Every dish is beautifully presented and packed with flavour, clearly made with care and fresh ingredients. Whether you’re here for a casual dinner or a special celebration, this place delivers on every level. Highly recommended!

The momo here was absolutely delicious - so juicy and tasty! I would rate the momo 5/5 for taste. address: Koteshwor-32, Himali Marga, Beside Himalaya College, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text: This place is really nice and offers great service. I ate steamed momo here before, and it was juicy and delicious. On another day, I tried jhol momo, and I absolutely loved it! Since then, I have become a regular customer, visiting 2–3 times a week just to eat jhol momo because it tastes amazing. I haven’t found such delicious momo anywhere else, so I highly recommend this place. All the food items here taste great and are also affordable!❤️

review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
I had a great experience at this restaurant. The food was absolutely delicious, fresh, and beautifully presented. Every dish had great taste and quality. The service was outstanding – the staff were very friendly, polite, and professional. They made sure everything was perfect and always checked if we needed anything.
I was also very impressed with the room service. It was fast, well organized, and delivered with a great attitude. The staff showed excellent hospitality and made us feel very comfortable.
The overall atmosphere was clean, relaxing, and welcoming. You can really see that the team works hard to provide the best experience for their guests. I truly appreciate the effort and professionalism of the whole team.
I highly recommend this place to anyone who loves good food and excellent service. I will definitely come back again.

You can have sit in as well as table and chair. The atmosphere is good but it feels a bit congested. The chicken biryani was good. Hot wings were way too hot. But they won't let you eat in peace(😜😜) as the waiters visit you too frequent.

Biryani taste good. Service is worst at night time, there won't be even anyone to give you water 😤
Price was too higher than it should be 😡😤

The whole grill chicken was fantastic and more delicious ,we enjoyed .
Please watch our video on yt channel :Pooja Baraily.
Thank you!

Pizza was hard like a biscuit.
Biryani was nice.

I recently visited this place and had an okay experience, but there is definitely room for improvement. While the ambiance is quite nice and provides a good vibe for a meal, several factors held back the overall experience.
The Downsides:
Hygiene Issues: I was quite disappointed to see flies hovering around the table. It felt very unhygienic and made it difficult to enjoy the food comfortably.
Service: The staff wasn't very proactive. We had to constantly call them over for every little thing. Ideally, when guests are present, the staff should be attentive and check in to see if anything is needed. That level of hospitality was unfortunately missing.
Food: The food was average—nothing particularly stood out, though the presentation was decent.
Overall: It’s a good spot for the atmosphere, but the management needs to focus on pest control and training their staff to be more attentive to guests.

Samir Limbu served us very well, polite and attentive. Every dish we tried was delicious. The place has a cozy atmosphere and is perfect for family gatherings also. They also offer a nice garden area, bar space, and separate seating options, which makes it even better. Parking was sufficient and convenient.

Highly recommended!

This is easily one of the best place for sekuwa in kathmandu. We had tried pork sekuwa several times and I must say its the best dish.
The while fish is also ine of my favourite dish.
The ambience is very good.
The customer service is awesome.
Recommended for everyone

Went to Michael Grill in Mid-Baneshwor. The ambience was good, and the staff were friendly and welcoming when we entered. We ordered a couple of dishes, but there was no consistency in the food. We ordered the same items, yet the taste was different in each dish.
Excellent place for food.Menuka provided excellent and friendly service. Very attentive and professional. Thank you for the great hospitality

Prices are cheaper and food is very good.

Food taste great.

One of the nicest places to be especially if you want to have a light snacks like coffee and cookies or sandwich. Some of the recommended items are :
1. Chocolate cookies - one of the best I have
2. Grilled veg sandwich - tasty
3. Hot chocolate - tasty

I did a 15-day Barista training here and honestly, it was such a good experience. The trainer was amazing—super helpful, friendly, and really good at teaching. I never felt shy to ask questions, and every session was fun and full of learning.

The space itself feels so homely and comfortable, which made the training even better. It didn’t feel like a strict classroom, more like a place where you can actually enjoy learning.

In just 15 days, I learned so much and also gained confidence in making coffee. I’m really happy I chose this place and would definitely recommend it to anyone who wants to learn barista skills in a positive and supportive environment.

-Very good ambience.

Kind like everything, will visit again

Looking for a cozy space to chill, work on your own or hangout with friends? Would highly recommend this space. The ambience is extremely cozy and welcoming and the drinks menu varied yet food menu limited. The prices would say okaish and the espresso tonic was quite good.

I rarely visit coffee shop, but the moment I entered the place, I was completely taken by it. The simple décor - with the ceiling adorned with ornaments made of twigs - drove me a little crazy in the best way.
I’m not much into coffee, but the atmosphere was so intense and overwhelming that it made me feel like coffee was something missing from my life.
Thank you, and I will definitely visit again.
one of the best newari khaja ghar in this area. you should definitely taste their khaja set, sapumicha and mixed achaar. aile is good quality. prices are affordable and reasonable.

food near my workstation

Everything here was amazing as always! I really enjoy visiting this restaurant because the food, atmosphere, and overall experience never disappoint. This visit felt even more special because the staff service was outstanding. Everyone was kind, welcoming, and attentive throughout. The staff member I took a selfie with was especially wonderful — super friendly, professional, and made us feel very comfortable. Great food with even better hospitality. Highly recommended!
Excellent food and great atmosphere. The staff were friendly and attentive, and the service was quick. The flavors were authentic and everything was well prepared. Highly recommended!"

Absolutely loved this place!
Really cosy vibe, super friendly staff, and top-notch food. Everything was fresh, full of flavour, and nicely presented. Would definitely recommend it to anyone after a great dining experience.

— Dipesh Thapa, Sindhuli (SindhuliMart)

Well, few goods and few areas for improvement.
1. Finding a car parking in this location is an instant plus. Though only space for 4-5 cars, it is a huge feel good factor.
2. Ambience is just so so. Tin roof, untidy cloths hung underneath, ununiform chairs, sofa and tables. Also, tables had patches of burnt holes, glass tables broken. Overall vibe gave quite cheap feel.
3. Cleanliness of partition glasses and toilets are also below par.
4. Wait was a bit too long depite nit being rush hour.
5. We had proper meal set here. Rice and dal was good. Curry was served cold! Also chicken curry reminded me an old advert "Khaali noon ra pyaaj haalnus, baaki kaam meat masala le garchha!" No effort put in chicken curry.

Final verdict?
If ambience is not an issue and you want to grab quick snack or couple of beers, this place is Yes!
If want proper ambience for cosy gathering or family time, this place is No!

The place is good and decent. It's nothing special but good enough for a casual hangout between friends. The food items labelled as Timmure are delicious but you definitely need to drink to wash away the strong taste of Timur. The best time to visit this place is at 6 pm.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
I had a wonderful dining experience at House of Thakali. The food was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The traditional Thakali set was authentic and well-balanced, with delicious side dishes and warm hospitality.
The restaurant's atmosphere was clean, comfortable, and welcoming, making it a great place to enjoy a meal with family and friends. The staff were attentive, courteous, and ensured that guests felt valued throughout their visit.
I highly recommend House of Thakali to anyone looking to enjoy authentic Nepali and Thakali cuisine. Thank you for the excellent food and service.
– Prabha Maharjan 🌿🍽️

I visited House of Thakali while I was in Nepal for a holiday. The ambiance, the professionalism of the staff, and the food ,everything was just perfect. We visited the place three times during my 15-day stay in Kathmandu.
A special mention to their staff member, Aakash,he was sweet, polite, and very professional. I will definitely visit again when I return to Nepal next year. What a place, what amazing food absolutely loved it!

Great food with truly authentic flavors , the kanchamba was on point and the thakali set was delicious. The place is clean, aesthetically pleasing, and has a warm, welcoming vibe. The staff were very friendly and supportive; even when we accidentally made a mess, they quickly cleaned it up without any hesitation and reassured us. Definitely a restaurant worth visiting!

The service was amazing.
All the side dishes were done just right.
The staff come and check you every often and very attentive.

I visited House of Thakali. The simple White House ambiance, the professionalism of the staff, and the food ,everything was perfect.
A special thank you for Bhagirath Regmi 🙏and very professional. I will definitely visit again, and recommend. Amazing Himalayan cuisine absolutely loved it!
Taste of Kathmandu
Great ambience with great music playing in the background.. Very aesthetic and pretty.. The food here is authentic and very fitting to the taste palette 🫶 It is already my second time here

It was good place to hangout with friends. It has games and the vibe is also very nice . We ordered momo and keema noodles which were yum but the burger was not good . The bun was dry and the meat was burnt .
Good and tasty food. Innovative taste. Creamy Momo was delicious. Fusion of momo, pizza and pasta.
Tried schezwan noodles…unique flavour for the nepali palette….sweet and spicy…will visit again for the new dishes.
Great place for lunch. One of the best noodle and momo I have had in Kathmandu. The unique creamy momo is a must try.

Stepping onto this rooftop cafe feels like a step back in time. The view of the ancient heritage buildings is breathtaking, offering a serene escape from the city buzz. It's the perfect spot to unwind, with a fantastic ambiance and a stunning panorama that makes every sip of coffee feel like a moment to cherish.

Fine place for a nice cup of coffee and a slice of cake. I got the blueberry cheesecake and I liked it. Although the cream cheese was not as the traditional one (looked more like thick wipped cream) it was still tasty.

I took a hot chocolate and piece of cheese cake. It came to over 600 rupees but the quality was very poor. The staff looked like they didn't care much & the place lacked character, creativity & atmosphere.

Sad there aren't better cafes in the area. The standard seems very poor compared to somewhere like Bouda.

We had lunch here in Bhaktapur and the experience was mixed. We ordered chicken, buff, and fish. The fish was good overall nothing amazing, but neutral and fine to eat. The chicken was average, nothing special. But the buff was really disappointing. It was extremely hard to chew, to the point where I couldn’t even swallow it I kept chewing and chewing but it just wouldn’t get soft.
The worst part was the rice. It was dry, cold, and rough not the usual texture at all. Overall, the food didn’t meet expectations. Hoping they improve the quality.:)
We enjoyed the food specially Nepali Veg and Chicken Thali. Kids are also enjoyed their food. Atmosphere is WOW 😮

We stayed at Namaste Bhaktapur Homestay and therefore we had breakfast here. The pancakes are amazing!! We also had a cooking class from the chef, who patiently taught us everything about Nepali flavours in Thali and curry. The chef’s flavours were great, even more since we got to put it in our own food. Highly recommend! Also the location is very good, with nice views from the terrace on Durbar sq!

I just went to Namaste Restaurant today.Here the hospitality, the traditional vibes and the delicious food really amazed me...

If you wanna have some peaceful time here in Bhaktapur. This place is must..

address: Dattatraya Square, Tachupal Tole, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text:
A delightful gem in Bhaktapur, offering not just great food but a beautiful ambiance. The atmosphere is really nice, with a peaceful green space right in the middle of the square. It’s a calm and relaxing place to enjoy your meal.

The food is really good, and they have plenty of tasty vegetarian options. I had the tofu pad Thai, and it was amazing….full of flavor and just the right amount of spice.

Overall, it's a fantastic dining experience with excellent service, delicious food, and a tranquil setting. Highly recommend for anyone visiting Bhaktapur!

address: Dattatraya Square, Tachupal Tole, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: A great and authentic little Guesthouse and Restaurant. Absolutely beautiful setting with authentic but contemporary and well thought through decor. Is just a nice place to hang out and eat and drink.  The menu isn't massive but what they do they do well.  The local spirits they talk about on the menu is Rakshi and is pleasantly flavoured, I think with Cardamom, which makes it nice to sip.. unlike some of the rougher unflavored stuff that I tasted in Bhaktapur. Prices are quite reasonable compared to De Temple.

address: Dattatraya Square, Tachupal Tole, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text:
I find myself coming back to dine at Mayur Restaurant once every two three months. The food is soooo scrumptious, and the ambiance very serene.

This is the only place in Dattatraya Square that is actually a 5/5. Truly exquisite!

address: Dattatraya Square, Tachupal Tole, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text:
This restaurant is located inside an ancient traditional courtyard with beautiful decorations. It is calm and quiet in contrast to the busy city.
Here you can get excellent food, the staff is really friendly, and you get to relax a little. If you don't like spicy food, they will actually give you non-spicy food, not like in many other places where "not spicy" still results in a fire in your mouth for the average Western tongue.
It is one of the best places I've eaten in Nepal so far.

address: Dattatraya Square, Tachupal Tole, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text:
We were really disappointed with our experience here. We ordered the chicken local thali and the red Thai curry with chicken and rice, and unfortunately, both dishes fell short—especially for the price. The chicken in the thali was overcooked. The daal in the thali was extremely watery, and the vegetables were undercooked. The red Thai curry was overly salty and had only three small pieces of chicken in it.

To make things worse, the service was poor. One of the staff members had a terrible attitude—when we raised concerns about the food, they showed no interest in helping, simply dropped the dishes on the table and walked away.

Overall, not worth the price or the experience.
Very nice place for lunch under sun after city walk. Local food is very good. Staff very nice. The restaurant is beautiful.

the ambiance of the property was really good but to be honest they needs to work on the taste of the food ... the food was not good at all ... if you want to pay for the ambiance then its fine else is a very expensive place
What a wonderful place! I enjoyed visiting there very much. Thank you. ❤️

I went there for a date, the place was really gloomy. COLD and filled with FUNGUS and spider net. We only had 1 plat momo that was soo expensive and it gave us food poisoning. I AM DYING with pain as i type this!!!
Good customer service. Too expensive. Food is basic. Go there for a tea not for food.

The best hotel in Nepal and one of the nicest places in the world. A unique, historical, spiritual setting with a magic view over Durbar Square, traffic free and in the mornings and evenings also tourist free. I have come back several times since 1992 (and travelled all over the world including most areas in Nepal). Since 2016 or 2017, the rooms are very well renovated with tiled own bathrooms, hot water and good pressure in the showers, bedlamps, good lightning in general, excellent wifi and practical nicely designed storages in the rooms.

The view and setting by Durbar Square is something you can not get tired of watching as well as the locals going to the closest temple (Shiva's) getting their tica and doing their hindu morning rituals early in the morning.

If you go to Nepal, you will probably pass the Kathmandu valley. My advice is not to stay in Kathmandu. It is nowadays terribly poluted from traffic, both regarding noise, pollution, and traffic jams. Stay at Shiva Guest House in Bhaktapur instead. You reach Kathmandu if you have/want to in half an hour but the air and atmosphere is like from another world.

We have only received good food and good service both regarding the room and the restaurant at Shiva GH. Their famous Curd with fruit and their Masala Pakod is highly recommended in particular and their lemon/ginger hot drink is better than elsewhere for some unknown reason.

Regarding comments on dogs in the area, we heard them in the evening around 9 pm for maximum 10 minutes barking. If you want it to be exactly like home, please stay at home.

Highly recommended for a night or two in Bhaktapur. Great food and a very helpful, friendly manager, Kailash.

Ask for a room overlooking the square - I had a fantastic view from the top floor.

And, as with everywhere in Nepal, bring ear plugs for the inevitable barking dogs at night.

We didn’t stay in hotel, we had food at restaurant. The Nepali thali was amazing, less oil, less spices and well cooked vegetables had won our hearts. We loved the decor, and keeping books on Nepal, Bhaktapur. One must try masala papad and Nepali thali here.
Stayed here 2 nights, very friendly host, even let me use his computer for some work I had to do. Food was good and for me, came fast.
Best hotel located inside Bhaktapur Durbar Square with nice cultural landscape view from hotel.

Servis and all workers were really nice and helpful.
My room was pretty small but I was alone and just for 1 night so it was OK. But the bathroom was even smaller which meant that with every shower even small fast one all the bathroom was wet... There were one more detail and that was missing scale for measure baggage weight.
Excellent place to experience authentic newari hospitality. Highly impressed with location, terrace view, food, customer service, and friendliness. Absolutely recommended. 💯

Gave it a 2 star because the ambience is nice. Staff are sweet. Food, especially the Newari set and pizza are down right awful. No taste in the Newari set and pizza is under cooked. We paid and ate elsewhere. I had high hopes and it’s such a nice looking place. Western tourists might like it but asians are going to be disappointed.

Food was decent, but service was very slow.

The full board meals are overpriced with almost the same amount of food as the half board
A great hotel to stay if you're in Bhaktapur. The food is excellent and the added bonus is the independent coffee shop. The best coffee I've had in Nepal.

This felt like a tourist trap experience. I would strongly recommend avoiding this restaurant.

The food was overpriced for the quality or taste, clearly targeting tourists. The thali was not good at all, and the veggie momo was just okay at best. On top of that, they charge an additional 4 percent card payment fee on the total bill.

What made it worse was that this lunch stop was entirely the tour guide’s decision, and she was not transparent about it in advance. The fact that the guide paid nothing for her own meal clearly indicates that this is a commission-driven restaurant targeting tour groups.

I don't usually write Google reviews, but this time I felt I had to.
I would not recommend this restaurant at all.
The prices are extremely high, and they even charge Rs. 65 for a bottle of water, which is more expensive than 5 star hotels . The cleanliness standards are also poor. If you are seated on the ground floor, you can clearly smell odors coming from the toilets, which makes the dining experience unpleasant.
In addition, the staff seem more interested in watching matches on TV than attending to customers and providing good service.
I understand that one review may not make much difference, and many business owners in Nepal may not pay attention to customer feedback. However, for long-term success, it is important to take customer concerns seriously and improve service quality, cleanliness, and overall customer experience.

The restaurant feels too geared toward Western tourists, which takes away from the authentic local charm. Unfortunately, the food reflects this too—lacking the traditional flavors and character you’d hope to experience.

We visited this cozy little rooftop restaurant tucked away behind the iconic Nyatapola Temple. The rooftop seating, overlooking the bustling streets of Bhaktapur, provided the perfect spot to relax and soak in the lively atmosphere.

For food, we ordered Buff Fried Rice, Buff Chatamari, Chicken Thakali (not pictured), and ended the meal with the traditional Newari dessert, Yomari.

The Buff Fried Rice had a pleasant smoky flavor from the wok, but considering the price, the quantity of buff pieces was a bit underwhelming. On the other hand, the Buff Chatamari was excellent. The soft yet slightly crispy base, topped with flavorful buff mince, paired beautifully with the chutney, making it a delightful experience. The Chicken Thakali was also quite satisfying.

The real star of the meal, however, was the Yomari. They serve two Yomaris per plate, each traditionally filled with either Chaku or Khuwa. We opted for one of each. The Chaku-filled Yomari was exceptional — rich, caramelized, and absolutely delicious. Unfortunately, the Khuwa version didn’t quite live up to my expectations. In hindsight, I wish I had ordered both with the Chaku filling!

We had our lunch form here during our Bhaktapur visit.
I had a simple veg sandwich and lemon mint drink.
The paneer pakora was good and the veg Momo's were good.
Only thing is the washroom was quite disappointing.

Food is good. Not that spicy, portions are big, prices ok. A lot of options with tofu. Good for vegetarian.

Have a Space on roof top (but a lot of smokers) and privat rooms downstairs.

Chinese food  in bhaktapur.  We felt it average on taste. The atmosphere and cozy sitting place is a plus point .
Minus point is The Price to Food ratio feels bit higher for locals like us.
Highly recommended if u are trying chinese here at Bhaktapur. Besides the ambience of roof top restaurant is chill and good.
Authentic chinese restaurant and food tastes good
Roof Top is good. New Management with Chines test is best.

We really enjoyed the food here. We ordered the hot and sour soup and the fried noodles (chow mein) and everything was top notch 🍜 The flavors were great and the portions were satisfying. It’s a simple spot, but the quality of the cooking is impressive. The staff was friendly and the service was quite fast 🥢 If you want a tasty and authentic meal for a fair price, this is a great choice 🥘
Balakhu food point offers standard Nepali fare at good price point. Tad bit spicy and salty so best to advice at time of ordering if you need to adjust according to own taste buds

I have tried a lot of fried rice in town. Like patan and kathamandu. But this is the best fried rice I have ever had. And the price is amazing. The way they cook and flip that I know will be delicious.

We had the very last dinner of our 3 week Nepal trip at this place and wow, I wish we had discovered it sooner. It was one of the best meals we had! Loved it so much. Service was also very nice, open and friendly. And quick! I definitely recommend going here.

We ate and bought took away here some times, and even bought to take to the airport for the last leg:) That's being said, it's cheap and tasty. Local customers they came. Staffs are friendly always. Really recommend.

I really loved it when they introduce choila fries . Best taste and of course we cannot miss their tornado fries.
On my last visit it was nice not best they can improve their keema noodles as well as customers service as well.

The place is crowded so you will have to wait. I visited there on saturday so that maybe the case. The potato fries with cheese loaded were also a good one with paneer. Overall loved the spiral potatoes with sauce crunchy.

We had a really disappointing experience here. After finishing our food, we found a cockroach at the bottom of the dish. When I pointed it out to the owner, he apologized and offered to prepare another one, but by then we had already eaten and it was too late. His explanation was that the café had been closed the day before and just reopened, which honestly isn’t an excuse for such poor hygiene.

Hygiene in a food place is basic. This isn’t what I expected when I came here. I really hope the owner takes this seriously and fixes it. If you plan to visit, just be extra careful.

Been here twice in 2 years. I noticed a fall in their taste and quality the 2nd time but it is still worth trying out. Don't order tornado fries though. It's popular here, but its just a glorified overpriced french fries with average taste.

As tempting as it looked, it was honestly disappointing. We ordered tornado fries and loaded chicken fries. The tornado fries were good, but the loaded chicken fries were terrible. The chicken was rubbery and unpleasant , we couldn’t finish it and ended up throwing it away.

address: North side of Dattatraya Square, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: This cafe and restaurant tends to be busy, and it's not that it isn't good but it's not remarkably better than other places in the square and is about 20% more expensive. I think it sees more business because it reserves tables for larger groups and so looks busy .. drawing more people in. At the time of our visit several items on the menu that this find elsewhere were not available Travellers Bistro across the street is less busy and in my view the food is just as good. Also for dinner, Mayur which is also across the street has good authentic Nepali and also international options in a nice setting, although the menu is much smaller.

address: North side of Dattatraya Square, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text:
We recently dined at De Temple Café, located right next to Dattatreya Temple. The view of Dattatreya Square from the café is truly beautiful and makes it a great spot to sit and enjoy the surroundings.
However, I was quite disappointed with the food and service. We didn't receive even the basic level of hospitality that one would expect from such a place. What made me feel even more uncomfortable was the noticeable difference in how local guests and foreign guests were treated. It felt unfair and unwelcoming.
I hope the management pays attention to this and improves both their service quality and guest experience, as the café itself has great potential because of its location and view.

address: North side of Dattatraya Square, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: Nice local food! Nice location in main square with perfect view of square and temple.

address: North side of Dattatraya Square, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text:
A quiet haven in Dattatraya Square. Beautiful, kind staff that are ready to help you beyond what is needed.
Smooth, delicious coffees and the best mocha I've had in a long time!
Stunning views and not much traffic noises either. Highly recommended:)

address: North side of Dattatraya Square, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: The food was very good. We can recommend the chicken momos, the pad Thai, and spicy sour soup. The view of the Temple and square is amazing. A star deduction for service. They didn't tell us no cards when we came in and they had difficulty bringing hot sauce in a timely fashion.

address: Rooftop Of Hotel Garuda, Taumadhi Square, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: Lovely rooftop restaurant bar with tasty food, great service and unbeatable view of the Nyatapola Temple square and surrounding mountain panorama in the distance. Really enjoyed the fantastic fried momos and corn fritters, paired with ice cold beer. Such a great find. Credit cards accepted.

address: Rooftop Of Hotel Garuda, Taumadhi Square, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text:
I guess it's the lady from the reception. She was sitting outside doing some sun bathing and then when I entered inside the hotel she asks me in Nepali, "K ko lagi hola? (Eng: Why are you here?)". Shouldn't it be obvious why someone goes to a hotel? I had to repeat my answer to her and I said I'm here to eat. That was a very weird interaction. When she should be greeting the guests and welcoming them, she was behaving the other way around. The customer service should change.

And then the price of Newari Buff Khaja set is also very high -Rs. 700 for a plate. Not nice. However, the food is ok and the view from the rooftop is absolutely beautiful. I might not come here again though.

address: Rooftop Of Hotel Garuda, Taumadhi Square, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: Stayed at the adjacent hotel. Had a wonderful diner here thanks to the umatched panoramic view. Food and service were great, prices were good, a lot of choices on the menu. Would recommend eating here, even if you do not stay at the hotel.

address: Rooftop Of Hotel Garuda, Taumadhi Square, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: The quality and amount of food is appreciable. View from terrace is amazing. But ground floor sitting area needs more aesthetic decor, must complimenting the Bhaktapur tradition. The staff is courteous and humble. They also provide discount on liquor. They accept visa debit card.

address: Rooftop Of Hotel Garuda, Taumadhi Square, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: Location is amazing but I think they should have done a lot more in food quality. Sorry to say but this is one of the worst places to eat that I've experienced. Maal bhako chaal nabhako place. Best wishes that this place gets improved in future.

I went there to have Thakali and Pakoda, but the service was not worth the price at all. The food quality and service were disappointing compared to the amount charged.
On top of that, they added a 10% service charge in the bill, even though the service was poor and not satisfactory. Customers should receive good hospitality if extra service charges are applied.
Overall, very bad restaurant experience — overpriced food, slow service, and unfair billing. Not recommended. Please improve your service quality.
Nice food good service n nice place

Horrible service, horrible price, don't make the mistake of coming here. Everything is more than double the price lmfao. Definitely not worth it at all. Thukka bhikhari haru alikati laaj lagdaina lmao 😂
Amazing ambiance and delicious food with clean view of durbarsquares main gate . One of the best choice in bhaktapur to spend some time
The all food is good as well as the service.

I recently visited Bhaktapur Durbar Square with my wife and her friends, and it was a wonderful experience! The historical architecture and the intricate wood carvings are truly breathtaking—it feels like walking through a living museum.

The overall atmosphere is serene and culturally rich. However, I felt that the presence of too many shops inside the main compound slightly took away from the authentic historical vibe. It would have been even better if the area was kept more clear of commercial stalls to let the monuments shine.

Still, it’s a must-visit spot if you’re in the Kathmandu Valley. Great for group photos and soaking in Nepalese heritage!

Amazing and beautiful! I would go back and spend more time there! The buildings and layouts of the town are so well maintained, unique and historical. As soon as I stepped in there, I just felt so much history! What amazed me was that the buildings are not only for display to the tourists, some of the local people actually live in these super old and original houses!
Nice vibes and delicious food!

Bhaktapur Durbar Square is like stepping into a living museum of Nepal’s rich history and craftsmanship. The moment you enter, you’re surrounded by beautifully preserved temples, intricate wood carvings, and centuries-old architecture that reflect the glory of the Malla era. Every corner feels like it has a story to tell.

The highlight is definitely the 55-Window Palace and the Vatsala Temple, along with the iconic Nyatapola Temple—Nepal’s tallest pagoda-style temple. Climbing up its steps gives a great view of the square and a sense of the grandeur of traditional Nepali design.

What makes Bhaktapur special is that it’s not just a tourist spot—it’s still a thriving town. You’ll see locals going about their daily lives, potters shaping clay in Pottery Square, and artisans continuing age-old traditions. The atmosphere is calm and less chaotic compared to Kathmandu, making it perfect for a relaxed exploration.

The cleanliness and preservation of the area are impressive, and the entry fee clearly goes toward maintaining this heritage site. Try the famous “Juju Dhau” (King Curd) while you’re there—it’s a must!

Overall, Bhaktapur Durbar Square offers a perfect blend of history, culture, and authentic local life. It’s an absolute must-visit for anyone traveling to Nepal.

One of my favorite places in Nepal. Every minute spent here feels special. The atmosphere is amazing, with beautiful temples, historic streets, and friendly locals.
Make sure to try the famous King Curd (Juju Dhau), visit a rooftop café, and explore the countless small shops. It's a perfect place to walk around, relax, and enjoy local culture.
Highly recommended!

Bhaktapur Durbar Square is truly a masterpiece of ancient art and architecture. The beautiful traditional designs, detailed wood carvings, and incredible stone art are simply unbelievable. Every temple and structure tells a story of history and culture.
The environment is very peaceful and calm, making it a perfect place to walk around, explore, and appreciate the craftsmanship of our ancestors. The fine wood art on the windows and doors and the stunning stone sculptures show the high level of skill from ancient times.
It’s a must-visit place for anyone who loves history, culture, and breathtaking architecture. Truly one of the most beautiful heritage sites in Nepal.

An average dining experience overall. The service was okay-nothing to complain about, but nothing that stood out either. The food was decent and well-prepared, but it didn't feel particularly special or memorable. It's a fine place if you're nearby

We looked at 3 different menus in the area and all were expensive and bad veggie options. Then De Arena saved us!! Super good value, huge menu and everything was delicious. Chairs are comfy, staff are great. We came in just wanting a snack and ended up having 3 courses and a drink. We leave with happy tummies and no regrets. There is also a pond with pretty fishies, yay 😊
Delicious food that made us come back again and again! Great value for money! The refillable Thalis are fantastic!
Good service, delicious 😋 food and peaceful environment 🤤 thank you for you good service's

Just had an unpleasant experience there.
We had 5 meals, 2 of them were unsatisfying.
Mix veg curry was "swimming" in oil and Kati rolls were the worst in our life. Just chopped vegetables with ketchup in though wrap.
The worst thing about this was that they brought the food for my wife after 25 minutes after my food so we ate separately.
Anyway staff and surroundings are nice.

Very nice place. Will happily make tofu dishes for you (menu doesn't list tofu, but it is available for many meals, e.g. bibimbap).
Friendly staff

This place is really peaceful as it's name says! Very quiet environment at the dattatraya square itself, you won't feel like you are this near to the main street of Bhaktapur. Not quite a fan of Korean dishes, yet tried some and it was worth it. They also have other menu than Korean as well. It's definitely a must go to place if you are into Korean dishes, nice ambience and a quiet environment. The service was also good. We enjoyed the evening.

I recently dined at Peaceful Restaurant with my sister, and it truly lived up to its name. The atmosphere was warm and welcoming, with soft music and tasteful decor that made it feel like a little escape from the busy city. It was really Peaceful.
The food was the highlight - every dish was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. I tried the Bibimbap, Kimbap, Korean BBQ, and all the dishes were outstanding. The kimchi had just the right amount of spice, and the side dishes (banchan) were generous and delicious.
The staff were friendly and attentive, always making sure we were comfortable without being intrusive.
They even explained some of the dishes to us, which added to the experience.
Whether you're a fan of Korean cuisine or trying it for the first time, Peaceful Restaurant is definitely worth a visit. I'll be coming back for sure!
Probably best korean food in town , it was bit  crowded yesterday however our food got served at right time, great ambience and peaceful environment as its name, good price , will visit again.

We visited this lovely Korean restaurant a week ago. The food was amazing, and the waitress was so kind and helpful. She even told us how to properly eat bibimbap by mixing it together and  to wrap samgyeopsal in lettuce with other side dishes — we’ve never enjoyed Korean food this much! Her care and patience really made our visit special. Definitely worth visiting!

I had an amazing late breakfast at this place. First of all I got a very comfortable place to sit, at the window on a nice couch. Second of all is the food, just amazing. I got the smoothie bowl, a lassie, americano and the mung bean pancake with salmon and poached egg. I enjoyed every bite of it! I forgot to make a photo of the menu, but they have a bigger menu card than written on chalkboard outside.

I got a buddha bowl with an extra egg.
The tofu was not good and small portion but otherwise all the veggies and quinoa was super tasty delicious ! So happy to get the chance to eat a balanced meal
Very friendly staff and comfy place
The place is cosy and nice with gorgeous newari architecture. The food however was only okay.
Went there with school mates fos dashai gathering. Food and ambiance is fine for gathering.

Such a great cafe and people. Do yourself a favor and take a visit here. You can do pottery here, can buy made in nepal ceramic items, drink coffee/tea or eat light bites & desserts. Coffee was great and they also had many items for sale as i have posted here.

Tina and Poonam were very lovely as well as helpful. Pottery brothers were really kind and generous, guided us from start to finish while doing pottery. All of the staff were truly amazing.🤗

They also have rooftop restaurant upstairs with a great view in which you can enjoy your meal and relax.

Forgot to mention, there was variety of desserts and coffee items available in the cafe. It’s great to give a visit.☕️🍰

address: Bhaktapur Multiple Campus Road, Dudhpati-1, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: Perfect place for coffee and food alongside Bhajya Pukhu pond view. Burger and Sanwich are Highly recommended! Please don't miss it. 🙌🏻 Probably the best Burger & Sandwich in Bhaktapur and K-Town. I still crave for it when I think about it 😜

address: Bhaktapur Multiple Campus Road, Dudhpati-1, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: miloco is a best place hangout with friends, fried rice is good and delicious and best place to eat and hangout.

address: Bhaktapur Multiple Campus Road, Dudhpati-1, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: One of the nicest place to chill and the food never disappoints. The restaurant is also a hangout spot for local bikers and the owners themselves occasionally organizes bike rides throughout the valley.

address: Bhaktapur Multiple Campus Road, Dudhpati-1, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: It's a chill place to hangout. We just ordered tea and Americano so can't comment about the food menu. We loved the place anyhow.

address: Bhaktapur Multiple Campus Road, Dudhpati-1, भक्तपुर 44800, Nepal
review_text: I often go there because the taste of dishes are so amazing and  i find it affordable too.

It was the best bara . AMA is too sweet and caring . Egg bara price is Rs 100 and mixed bata is 124 and Alu is 50 . She gave us discount also .
Please have some cash with you they don’t have fonepay. You will be asked if you want jhol or not it’s important that you say yes or try normal at first and add jhol after that . It is game changer .

Mixed bara had a really unique taste and texture due to her particular mix of ingredients. Others have commented on what the ingredients and ratios are. Spend some time enjoying the food, ambience, action and pay respects in the inner courtyard. If you’re near the square, it’s a must do for Bhaktapur.

The best Bara ever!

After watching so many reviews on insta, I went there to try Bara 2 years ago and till date, no any Bara has ever reached upto its level, for me.
Every time I visit Bhaktapur Durbar Square, I make sure not to miss this spot. It has become an absolute favorite of mine.
The aaloo is also tasty.

Once, I ate 4 plate Bara in a go, I love it that much!

A small place where Aama cooks Bara and other food items are also available. If you are lucky you'll find a place to sit, otherwise you'll have to stand and eat. We tried Chicken Egg Bara and it was delicious. Aloo was okayish. The price was a bit high though.

Nestled in Bhaktapur, Ama ko Bara Pasal stands out as a celebrated bara shop, drawing attention from local and international food bloggers alike. My repeated visits—this being the 3rd or 4th—have only reinforced the notion that consistency is the key to its culinary success. What sets this place apart is the presence of "ama," the lone chef, who tirelessly prepares each order, ensuring that the taste remains unwaveringly delightful over the years.

The ambiance is a lively spectacle, with the small space bustling with activity. There's a certain charm in the compact setting that adds to the overall experience. Whether you're a regular or a first-timer, the welcoming atmosphere and the aroma of Newari delicacies make it a must-visit spot for anyone with an appreciation for authentic and flavorful local cuisine. Ama ko Bara Pasal truly encapsulates the essence of Newari food in every bite, making it a gem in the heart of Bhaktapur.

The price was very local despite the view and the cleanness feeling of this place. We ate here twice, once for lunch, once for dinner and enjoyed it all. The entrance is a little bit hard to find but just follow google map and you'll got it. The staffs are nice and local customers there were nice too. Really recommend for anytime.

Waited 30+ minutes for a momo and the biryani never came. Atleast they could have said it will take time.
It once was a good place for food with good view. But has degraded significantly, the service has gone downhill, rudely talking front staff and not so good food as well.

Wonderful service and food! The food came really quickly. There is also a variety of seating options. We chose the seats at the very top, small but cute area. Food was delicious with great portions for its price! Would come back again

We had lunch and dinner a few times during our stay in Bhaktapur. The service is very good and the prices are good too. The food is freshly made but a little salty and not the best in Nepal. The place is hygienic and in the evening it is cosy.

The veg pokada is a good pick!

Great rooftop view over pottery square, so not many other tall buildings inhibiting the view. The food is nicer and at a lower price than many competitors in the area. The seating arrangements are neat and the overall place was tidy. Service was good and the food came in 10-15 minutes. Great spot 👌
Authentic Newari Ambiance and delicious food. Loved it. And staffs are so friendly and we'll behaved. Highly recommend.
We really enjoyed the food and the views. Thanks
The butter chicken was so great. even the egg is very well cooked, super soft!
Amazing! Best Momos …
Sunil Tamang is very good waiter.

restaurant_name: khwapa chhen Guest House and Restaurant
review_text: The location and rooftop of this place is quite great , we can view whole Bhaktapur palace and other Bhaktapur area too. And the decoration is so decent , with modern and newari traditional touch. The newari khaja set was good and hygienic , but potato was raw but they changed it. But they again forgot to keep the salt again it was kinda funny. The hospitality was very good.

restaurant_name: khwapa chhen Guest House and Restaurant
review_text: I did not stay there, but enjoyed the great view to Durbar Square during my perfect lunch. 🙏

restaurant_name: khwapa chhen Guest House and Restaurant
review_text: We spent one night here during our trip trough Nepal. The position is fantastic and the bedrooms are nice but it was definitely too cold. They don’t have any kind of heating and they make us have dinner in a dining room without real windows. We were frozen. Not suggested during the winter season.

restaurant_name: khwapa chhen Guest House and Restaurant
review_text: We didn’t stay in this hotel but the food there is perfect! Curry recommended!

restaurant_name: khwapa chhen Guest House and Restaurant
review_text:
I was with a small tour group, and ate lunch at the rooftop restaurant here. It affords a very nice view of the Durbar Square and of the Bhaktapur area in general. While enjoying our lunch, we were also treated to the sight of lots of kites flying from various rooftops.

And the food was quite good -- especially the local specialty dessert yogurt.

The food was really good and the atmosphere was nice, but the restaurant seemed quite understaffed. The staff who were working were polite and doing their best, they were clearly handling too many tables at once.

We had a very nice experience here when we came for dinner. Food and drinks menu was extensive and very good quality. Nice atmosphere, especially with the live music and outdoor ambience. The servers were also all very friendly, especially Gaurab Rai, our server.

I would recommend to others who want good food and vibes outside main Bhaktapur square. Thank you for accommodating us.

It’s a hidden gem for someone who like peaceful atmosphere with music and a good food😍😍 Me and my family had a good experience here. I would love to visit again. Staff was very polite and joyful as well 😊
We were there for a family celebration and it was really nice time. The ambience is really good, the food as well. The service of Gaurav Rai bhai was really good, he was attentive ever smiling.

Great food, clean and cozy environment, and friendly staff. The dishes were fresh and full of flavor, especially the [your favorite dish]. Service was quick and the prices are reasonable.

👉 Highly recommended! 👍

What a wonderful find.  Authentic atmosphere,  fabulous food, inexpensive, localed in someone 's home. Chill a bit... they make from scratch so a little patience will be greatly rewarded.

Top shelf eating experience!

🤩😍🤩
Good food, good atmosphere, good vibe, the best newari dish  I have ever had. 😋
Very friendly! Very testy and cheap! Strong recommend!
Never did i wait so long for my food. I waited 1h20 for a thali set and did not even get a refill. Very sad experience
Amazing traditional Indian - Nepali food for very reasonable price. Tasty Bara. Owners super friendly. Terrace available with nice view. Would definitely go again.

The space was welcoming and so well, making it ideal for gatherings and other pursuits. In a nutshell, Time's Cafe & Restro met my expectations to a measure. Some aspects of the cafe need to be upgraded, while others are good.   From this restaurant, the view of Kamalpokhari was stunning. This is a wonderful area to spend time with your loved ones. A wide range of delectable cuisine items is provided. Recommended.

Food tasted nice. It was friday eve with live music. The technical problem in speaker coudnt lower the volume. So it was quite unpleasant due to high volume. The band was talented and nice. We have good time with family.  Chicken chilly, buff momos, chiciken momos, friedrice, chowmein , chicken lollipop, pizza all were good . 👍

I love momos and thukpa of this place. Place was warm and well organized place for gathering and others. In simple term Time's cafe reached my expectations up to some extent. Some part of cafe is required for improvement but then it's okay  in others sections. Rest rooms weren't managed well and hygiene wasn't as good as others restaurant. The view of kamalpokhari from this restaurant was amazing. If you're having private time then i would recommend this place for lunch.
This place is perfect for good view of kamalpokhari.Loved the decoration of the place from wall painting,lights to prayer flags.Food is also good and staff are nice and friendly.
Warm atmosphere. Food is awesome. Staffs are awesome as well. Definitely recommended.

The best pizza in 📍 Bhaktapur. Myself being a resident of Bhaktapur, I really enjoy having Pizza of The North Rooftop. The cheese, the texture and the toppings are mouthlicious! Apart from pizza, rest of the foods are also good i.e.Cheesy Fries. Could work on staffs since they are little inattentive. Seating space is limited. P.S The View is amazing as well.

Went by seeing an online review, but the female lady was rude to us, not answering us properly, also while asking they didn’t assist us. Also warm water is also not available there, we asked for C Momo but they told they only have cold water.

Bad experience we had:(( Not recommended

Just gave teo star coz of good place and atmosphere but service and food needs improvement 🙂 Also the service was very very slooow

I recommend you to think twice before going, seems like it’s a couple place, saw groups of couple hugging and having their good time in roof top:)

The food was good, but the staff behavior was extremely bad. They barely responded and seemed uninterested. Poor service overshadowed the food.Specifically male staff members behaved extremely poorly. He was unresponsive and unhelpful, which made the overall experience very unpleasant & ruined the visit.

A place with a chilled view with nice drink and good foods.
Would recommend chicken quesadilla and the fizzy drink made from Sprite (avoid the peach iced tea tho, it was bland and warm)

The Rooftop with a view of the hills. Located around Na:Pukhu, the North Rooftop is great to chill.

The food and beverages are okay-ish. I had ordered Hot Wings and a Hawaiian Pizza. The hot wings were purely spicy. For the beverage, I had cold cappuccino and fizz mint (something like that). Those were of average taste.

The rooftop is great if the wind blows else, it is hot af. 🥵

Tried this place recently and honestly loved the experience. The food was super tasty, especially the momo, sekuwa and foods with special timmur taste. The prices were really reasonable. Drinks were available at MRP which was a nice surprise. The ambiance was clean and cozy, perfect for hanging out with friends or family. Service was also very warm and quick. You can tell they genuinely care about customers. Definitely one of those places you’ll want to visit again. I visit 2-3 times in a month. Addicted to Timmure and its taste.

Food Just Melted My Soul!
Second time here. Honestly, last time food was average but their specialty Timmure chicken is unique. On my way to home i entered for a quick bite and this time the food is MIND BLOWING!!
MUST VISIT….. the foods got better and i will visit here often with friends and family. 5 star doesn’t justify the taste 🤤🤤🤤🤤

Timmure is a good place to hangout with friends and family members. The restaurant vibe is really good. Food and beverage is really good with limited menu options. Service is not satisfactory during busy hours.

You get drinks at MRP here and food prices is also reasonable not too cheep not too expensive. Overall dining experience is good. I would recommend to try timmure chicken and pork sekuwa if you’re non vegetarian and for vegetarian paneer pakauda.
love the hospitality and the coffee made by manish was good.  The service was nice and very friendly and respectful.
Love the Timure chicken so much!!!!The service is so swift and the staff is so kind. Must visit

A Hidden Gem with Stunning Views
I recently visited Hotel Empire & Rooftop Restaurant and was genuinely impressed. The highlight is definitely the rooftop—the view is incredible, especially all the focus intended is Nyatapol and scenic hills. The atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming, making it a perfect spot for evening drinks or a casual dinner.
​The staff were attentive without being intrusive, and the food arrived hot and well-presented. I highly recommend trying their Nasi Goreng and Pad Thai! Whether you are staying at the hotel or just dropping in for the rooftop experience, it’s well worth a visit.

I would honestly give this place a 0 rating. Please don’t even consider stepping in here. I went for some snacks and a Thali set, and it was completely disappointing.
For the first time, I saw a restaurant with no handwash station at all no proper soap, no separate area to wash hands. The only option was inside the bathroom, which was shared by both men and women. I can’t even imagine what the kitchen looks like because the entire restaurant felt unhygienic, and the bathroom was extremely dirty.
I went there because I was craving Thali, but I still regret it was that terrible.Never, ever visit this place.
The food was delicious. Had my client stay here and they highly appreciated the view from the room. Will recommend more clients.
Good food with great ambience. Staffs were very polite and the hotel was very clean and sanitized compared to other hotels around bhaktapur. Will definitely be back.
Cool place to hangout over Bhaktapur, Delicious food, warm hospitality & specially the rooftop view, I would love to visit this place again

I rarely leave bad reviews, but my experience at KV Lounge and Jagatiko Chhoila Pasal was genuinely disappointing. From start to finish, the customer service (waiter) was unprofessional and careless. He was enjoying hookah and was busy showing tricks to his fellow staff rather than helping the paying customers. He seemed completely disinterested and unhelpful, as if serving customers was an inconvenience.

The food took forever to arrive, and when it finally did, it was cold and tasted stale — definitely not worth the price. When I politely brought up the issue, the staff gave me attitude instead of trying to fix the problem. There was zero accountability, and I left feeling frustrated and ignored.

What’s worse, the overall atmosphere was chaotic and unorganized. Tables weren’t cleaned promptly, and it felt like nobody cared about maintaining basic hygiene or service standards.

I had high expectations given the hype, but this was a huge letdown. I won’t be returning, and I wouldn’t recommend KV Lounge or Jagatiko Chhoila Pasal to anyone who values good food and respectful service.

“The food was absolutely delicious, especially their Indian and Newari cuisine. A must-try has to be the Tandoori Momo and Naan paired with their flavorful curries. The drinks also came with so many varieties 😍

A true go-to spot—not just for the quality of food, but also for the serene pondside view. An amazing experience all around!”

We had a delicious meal with our Nepalese friends at KV restaurant.  Very nice friendly service.  The dishes were freshly made and very tasty.  The restaurant is located on the edge of the old center.  Entrance at the rear.  Prices are lower than around Durham square.  Also a very nice romantic roof terrace with lovely sofas.  Worth a visit!

Very disappointed experience, Such a bad experience. We waited for more than 1 hour for our naan. Every time we asked the staff about the order, they kept saying just 5 minutes but the food never arrived on time. It was very frustrating to keep waiting with the same response repeatedly. Better communication and service would have made the experience much better.

KV lounge has always been my best place to hangout. Views seen from terrace is really awesome. One can enjoy the best food with unforgettable service and nature.
All staffs are friendly and attentive. They do their best to feel you comfortable.

This is the best meal I’ve had in a LONG while. Everything from the flavors and local ingredients to beautiful presentations and the history behind each plate make this tasting menu a MUST TRY. They change the menu every couple of months to get the freshest ingredients during each season. The manager, Bibek, made the experience so enjoyable and memorable. Thank you! Will be back for sure every time I’m at Nepal.

Saw an ad of this place while I’m on the plane to Nepal and decided to give this place a try.
Price wise - totally worth it as it is a small fraction of the fine dining experiences I’ve had before.
Experience - Find it’s a bit cut short due to the explanation given by the server was not clear, sometime the voice projection were too soft, maybe this is something to improve on. Some dishes like the meatball also came to us in room temperature, not warm which baffled me a bit.

On that note, I always view fine dining experiences like this is like telling us patron a story behind every dish, but this time it felt like there isn’t any and I kept coming back to the menu to know more.

Ambiance - Deco was nice and traditional, and the restaurant is situated in the middle of housing areas but still quiet.
It was winter time and it does gets a bit chilly at night, which having the heater close was a relief, but once it moves around the warmth escapes you. Having said that it does affect the experience a bit.

Food taste: while I was quite excited to have my tastebuds tingled by strong spices and explosion of taste, I discovered that the dishes were made in almost earthy/natural taste-like. Maybe it’s Nepali cuisine -Tusa’s take but I doubt I’ll be braging more.
Overall the food is delectable, but doesn’t send me off smiling cheek to cheek.

However, I do applaud the effort to uplift local
Cuisine and wholeheartedly support what they are trying to do. I just had too much expectation!

The food here is delicious, but what impressed me even more was the chef’s intention and thoughtfulness reflected in each dish. The chef thoughtfully incorporates many elements of Nepalese culture into the food without overdoing it, allowing each dish to tell its own story. This truly left a lasting impression on me. The ambiance of the restaurant is also wonderful—cozy, calm, and welcoming. Overall, it’s an excellent experience and well worth the money. It’s a rare pleasure to enjoy food prepared with such care and passion.

I really would have liked to be delighted by this dinner in this restaurant in which I went especially to Bhaktapur from Thamel.
The overhaul experience was disappointing.
The restaurant is settled in a nice old historical building with a small courtyard.
The dishes are generally quite bland, even if an effort is made in their presentation.
We took the gourmet menu with the wine pairing for the sum of 12,000 nepalese rupees per person (approx 84 $ US).
It started with flavourless appetizer canapés. Then the consommé and its meatballs had very little flavour and was served warm. The river fish was also quite bland, far from what one could imagine, Nepal being full of varied spices.
The pork-based dish was better but nothing really impressive in terms of taste.
The wine was good.
In view of the price, the experience was therefore disappointing.

There is a restaurant tucked within Bhaktapur's UNESCO heritage streets that deserves the attention of the world. Tusa meaning "new beginning" is not merely dining; it is a manifesto enacted through food. Drawing from Nepal's 118 ecosystems and over 440 wild fruits and herbs, the kitchen transforms foraged ingredients into dishes that taste of this land and nowhere else. Every course tells a story of biodiversity, tradition, and sustainability. This is cooking that reaches backward into forests and traditional fields to create something genuinely forward-looking.

The New York Times has long celebrated restaurants that redefine culinary identity, and Tusa belongs in that conversation. It is a serious contender for Michelin recognition not because it mimics European fine dining, but because it achieves what the best restaurants everywhere aspire to: food that could exist nowhere else, prepared with technical precision and profound respect for place. In an ancient city, Tusa feels like the future.

I came on a recommendation. I found a beautiful room and the service was worthy of a good establishment.
I ordered yak croquettes and a lemon iced tea, to which they added a little extra sugar on the side in case I wanted it sweeter. (A nice touch.)
The portion of kibble was generous for a starter, which is nice considering the price.
The croquettes was very tasty.

I just love everything here!  The restaurant atmosphere is nice, the service is good and best the food is delicious!!! Personally I tried the carbonara and grill pork it’s so good! A IPA draft beer is a must for the hot day!
A perfect fine class dining in Bhaktapur. The atmosphere is warm and welcoming. The staffs are attentive.

Likely the high-end dining experience in Bhaktapur. It's in a nice setting with almost Western quality table settings and decor. The hotel atrium that we walked through also like really nicely appointed. The Newari set menu that we had was great and the pork belly starter was also good. They offered us more food in the same way as you would expect with a Dal Bhat, but less about carb volume and we had a good sized amount of small and tasty dishes, including buffalo, spinach, split peas, yogurt, flattened rice and other traditional items... Rakshi included.

Community at The Nanee is one of the youngest hotel and restaurants on the way at the narrow alley of Bhaktapur Durbar Square. It is amazingly constructed bearing in mind about the Vastu Bigyan. The art and architecture has been creatively designed by Renowned Mr Manish Lal Shrestha Ji. The local food is so satisfying and the international cuisine has maintained its standard. The staffs are welcoming wit smile and namaste. The kitchen is clean. The rooms are designed according to price and and has maintained its standard. Amenities in the rooms are nature friendly

I recently visited Tryst Fast Food Restaurant, located near Siddhapokhari, with my mother and sister, and it turned into a truly wonderful dining experience for all of us.

The ambience of the restaurant really caught our attention—it looks great from both the outside and inside. It has a clean, cozy vibe that made us feel relaxed and comfortable right away.

We were honestly surprised by how good the food and drinks were—way better than we expected! We ordered their Tryst Special Chicken Sizzler, Chinese Chopsuey, and Spicy Chicken. For beverages, we went with an Iced Americano and an Iced Latte.

The presentation of the food was impressive, and the way it was served showed great attention to detail. But most importantly, the taste was absolutely delicious—so much so that I felt like I had to share this experience through a review!

All in all, we had a great time at Tryst Fast Food. If you’re around Siddhapokhari and looking for a place to enjoy tasty food in a nice setting, I definitely recommend giving this place a try.

Today I visited a restaurant named Tryst Fast Food located at Siddhapokhari. I ordered Buff Sukuti Sadheko and a Chicken Pizza.

Buff Sukuti Sadheko was quite decent in taste , flavorful and enjoyable. However, I felt the quantity was not up to the mark considering the price. Could definitely be improved on that part.

As for the Chicken Pizza, honestly, it was a big letdown. It didn’t feel like a pizza at all. The base was more like a roti than actual pizza dough. If you’re someone looking for a proper pizza experience, I wouldn’t recommend trying it here.

Ambiance was good though , cozy and decent for a casual meal.

Overall, go for the sukuti if you’re around, enjoy the place, but definitely skip the pizza.

Located conveniently on Main Street, Tryst  is a go-to spot for a quick and satisfying meal. The ambiance is casual, with vibrant decor and ample seating  indoors. The menu boasts a variety of meat items , fries, and drinks, catering to diverse tastes. One standout feature of this restaurant is its commitment to quality ingredients. Vegetarian options are also available, ensuring there's something for everyone.
Service at Tryst is fast and efficient, perfect for those looking to grab a quick bite on their lunch break or between errands. The staff is friendly and accommodating, always willing to customize orders to suit individual preferences.

A nice place near Siddha Pokhari for fast food. The ambience is great. This place has sufficient seating spaces. The service is also fast and the food is awesome. DO visit when around Siddha Pokhari!!

The food tasted great, and we had leftover momos to go as we ordered too much food for four. There is a clean bathroom on each floor, and bike and car parking is across from it.

worst dining experience ever. ordered spicy potato wedges via foodmandu and this is what i recieved. no dipping sauce nothing, just the leftover potato cuts fried and packed.

What truly made the visit special was the stunning view of Bhaktapur that you can enjoy while dining. The peaceful scenery combined with the restaurant’s warm ambience created a relaxing and memorable atmosphere. Whether you’re going for a casual meal or a special outing, this place offers great food, beautiful surroundings, and a cozy vibe. Highly recommended!

So good , loved everything.. the ambience,.. the quality of food.. & the friendly staff . Worth giving try . It was first time going to alphabet pizzeria but they were so welcoming it felt like i was a regular customer.. so sweet and nice.. helped me suprise my best friend with complimentary decor which she was not expecting at all ..thank you❤️❤️

An Amazing Experience!

The pasta was cooked to perfection, with delicate and refined flavors.
The pizza was truly unique — featuring paneer cheese and a wonderful aroma of high-quality olive oil.
Abviously, we went for drings and dessert as well.
Above all, the service stood out — delivered by staff who clearly love what they do.

Amazing pizza, and the chocolate milkshake was good too! The pizza was almost like an Neapolitan pizza. You taste the difference in dough between a real Italian pizza and this one, but it was for sure the best I got in Nepal!  I dare to say the dough has been rising for +24 hours or so, as it should be, otherwise the dough wouldn't be so fluffy and light on the stomach. I recommend!
