What stood out even more was the exceptional service. The staff were extremely kind and helpful, ensuring we were comfortable throughout our visit. I was especially impressed by the manager, who personally visited every table to ask about the quality and taste of the food, the staff’s behaviour, and the overall dining experience. This level of attention and care truly elevated our visit.

I highly recommend everyone to visit Food Bowl Cafe and Restaurant at least once. You won’t regret your decision—it’s an experience worth having!

restaurant_name: Old Town Food: Best Late-Night Food in Bhaktapur
review_text:
I’ve been a loyal customer of Old Town for quite some time, and I’ve always appreciated their food quality and prompt delivery. However, my recent experience was extremely disappointing. While enjoying a paneer curry, I found a piece of chicken in the dish. As a vegetarian, this was not only shocking but completely unacceptable.

I immediately contacted the restaurant, and they promised to follow up within 20 minutes. It’s now been a few hours, and I’ve received no response. This complete lack of accountability is unacceptable and reflects poorly on their commitment to customer satisfaction.

Due to this experience, I will no longer be ordering from Old Town. Take a look at the photo attached

​A Masterclass in Nepalese Hospitality

The Nanee is more than just a hotel; it’s an experience. Walking through the candlelit hallway into the central atrium feels like stepping into a sanctuary. Our room was excellent—plenty of space for trekking luggage, a beautiful bathtub, and a terrace with views of the local community well and resilient historic architecture. We loved the communal dinner sitting at the long table, and the curated tours were expertly handled. The staff are the heart of this place: professional, warm, and always wearing a smile.

I took my mom and aunt for dinner. The food served was too good. The fusion of newari cuisine was very delightful to watch and taste. My mom and aunt were very satisfied. Overall, the experience was very good.

We recommend this wonderful restaurant as it is a very peaceful and lovely place. It will serve you not just high quality food yet so much more. The atmosphere is unique and The waitstaff Akash, Manjila, Mr jack are all wonderfully sweet and personable. They will love to serve you and  give you an amazing experience. We recommend the pumpkin soup which is rich and fully balanced, which comes with fresh homemade bread.  We also really enjoyed Nanee style noodle soup that comes with wonderful mushrooms on top.  The chef and the kitchen staff are providing beautifully curated meals that are clean and delicious. We highly recommend you come & enjoy  for yourselves.
Tell them you heard about it from Rockstar and Echo

The Nanee has been a stunning experience. The staff are utterly delightful and ensure your every need is met, including those you didn't know you needed, such as a personal dawn tour of the Square from the Manager! We visited in the midst of Dashain Festival, similar to our Christmas, when so many Nepali head home, making the warmth and hospitality of the Nanee team, even more heartfelt. As well as the staff, the rooms, food, design, and location were equally exceptional .. Get there people!

Staying at The Nanee feels like stepping back in time. The moment you step out, you are transported into centuries of rich history and heritage. The staff are extremely courteous, the rooms are comfortable, and the food is excellent. A perfect blend of old-world charm and modern comfort. Highly recommended.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Newa Lahana, and it was truly a delightful experience. The hotel beautifully blends traditional Newari architecture with modern comfort. The rooms were clean, spacious, and well-maintained, offering a warm and welcoming atmosphere.

The staff were exceptionally courteous and attentive, always ready to assist with a smile. The food was another highlight — authentic, flavorful, and served with great hospitality. The overall ambiance of the hotel made my stay both relaxing and memorable.

I would highly recommend Newa Lahana to anyone looking for a comfortable stay with a touch of cultural charm. I look forward to visiting again in the future.

I’ve been to this restaurant several times before with my friends, and we always loved it. The food used to be delicious, and the portions were very generous. That’s why we decided to visit again after a few months, expecting the same experience — but this time it was really disappointing.

We ordered our usual dishes, including buff sekwa (Rs. 450), but the portion was shockingly small — barely 7–8 tiny pieces of meat. Considering that raw buff meat costs around Rs. 600 per kg, the portion just doesn’t make sense. We even mentioned it to the server and cashier, but they didn’t seem to care or communicate properly.

To make things worse, the taste was nowhere near what it used to be. The food that was once full of flavor now feels bland and low-quality. It’s sad to see how much this place has gone downhill. Really hope they bring back the quality and quantity they used to offer.

The wings were below average in quality (with just 4 pcs), and the Mojito was overly sweet. Overall, the food was quite disappointing. The only saving grace was the nice view.
The food was below average and nothing really stood out. The only interesting part was the small adventurous staircase, apart from that, the place felt quite ordinary.

I had visited Matina Cafe in the past and had a reasonably positive experience. However, my recent visit was extremely disappointing, particularly from the perspective of cleanliness, hygiene, and overall restaurant management.
The approach to the first-floor seating area was noticeably dirty. The staircase and dining area appeared poorly maintained, with dirty tables and cigarette butts left around. The environment did not feel suitable for families, especially those visiting with children.
One of the most concerning aspects was the condition of the entrance area, where the toilet and hand-washing facilities are located. There was a strong unpleasant odor from the toilet, and the shared hand-wash area used by both restaurant patrons and toilet users was visibly unclean. This raises serious concerns about basic hygiene standards.
The dining area itself was uncomfortable due to unrestricted smoking. When customers smoke indoors, the space quickly becomes unbearable for non-smokers and particularly unsuitable for children and families.
The kitchen, located on the ground floor, also appeared extremely cramped and unhygienic. The sink was dirty, and food preparation practices were concerning. During my visit, momo dough was being prepared on top of a rusted refrigerator, with dough and wrappers directly exposed on the refrigerator surface. Such practices do not reflect acceptable food-handling standards.
Another issue was the apparent lack of separation between cleaning and food service duties. Staff members were observed cleaning dishes and floors and then serving food without any visible hygiene protocol in between. Basic restaurant hygiene practices, including proper handwashing and role separation, seemed absent.
Overall, the cafe appears to require significant improvements in cleanliness, food safety, staff training, and general maintenance. The location is excellent and has great potential, but the current condition falls well below the standards expected of a restaurant serving both local and international visitors.

The location of this cafe right opposite of the Nyatapola Temple in Bhaktapur is unbelievable. We sat on the 2nd floor balcony overlooking the historic square. What an amaxing view. The cold beer song with momos, local Newari sampler & noodle soup was delicious. 👏🏾👏🏾👍🏽👍🏽
The atmosphere is good. Foods are below average. The order took quite a good amount of time to arrive. Hopefully they improve in all areas. Best of luck.
Shree Gurpak Bhandar preserves the authentic taste of Bhaktapur by offering traditional Gudpak crafted with time-honored recipes and rich local flavors.”

It's a family run businesses since the generations n generations. You can feel the real taste of the ancient Newari dessert as it should be ( WITHOUT  any kind of chemicals ).
I will definitely go back.
HIGHLY RECOMMENDED !!!!!
Pure and homely taste. Good for health and happiness to family. Cheers!!!

The view from the rooftop is absolutely stunning, especially during the morning hour when the sunlight is soft and mellow. The hotel is just located around the corner of Durbar Square. The service is attentive, and the host speaks good english and can help you to arrange some tours at the hotel.
The room is okay, clean but small.
Breakfast is offered with the room rate.

Exceptional cleanliness and safety measures in place,made us feel secure during our stay at Taleju grand hotel.The view from rooftop was amazing and peaceful.Fooding and lodging hospitality was incredible. Highly recommended to visit Taleju Grand Hotel
Very hospitable staffs, they’re really kind and helpful. Location easy to find and closest to bhaktapur durbar square area. Recommend.
Damn good
Good food,free minaral water, good view

I recently visited Bambooze Corner Restaurant and Bar and had a pleasant overall experience. The food was enjoyable, and the highlight for me was the soup served with every dish. It was rich, flavorful, and perfectly complemented the meal. The soup alone is worth a visit and showcases the attention to detail in the kitchen.

The pork sekuwa, however, was average. While it was decent, it didn’t quite meet my expectations in terms of flavor and texture. It could use a bit more seasoning or a more refined cooking technique to truly stand out. Despite this, the rest of the dishes were satisfying and well-prepared.

One area that could use some improvement is the service. Although the staff was friendly and courteous, the service was slightly delayed. Faster service would elevate the dining experience significantly.

The ambiance of the restaurant was inviting and made for a comfortable setting to enjoy the meal. While there were a few areas for improvement, the quality of the food overall, especially the soups, made up for it. I’d still rate Bambooze Corner 4 out of 5 stars and recommend it for those seeking good food in a relaxed environment.

Newly opened in kageswori manohara planning.
Their full menu is still in verse.
Ample parking and located at quite space. The food needs some improvement. Service was good. The ambience in the night time was really appreciated.
Would definitely visit when the full menu is made. The pizza was good. Also the jhol momo.
Simple, warm and bright place to be. 👏

Even on a special day like Valentine’s Day, when we ordered food, they said items were already sold out. The service was absolutely zero. The food arrived only after 45 minutes, and even then it had no garnish and looked like it was from a local hotel. In the end, when we ordered chicken biryani—OMG—there was a long hair in it as well. All the food was extremely oily. The only thing I liked was the place itself, but the food and the service were the worst.
Overall, it was a very bad experience👎👎👎👎👎

FOOD is great. But lack the item on the menu. Service is good but due to lack of the manpower you might have to wait more time. Atmosphere during night to good. No live music. They use speaker for music. So Choice is yours.

Food was okish. Biryani was abit oily, but timur item just hit the right spot.
They didn't provide us any bills even after payment which is a big no for me for next time. Ambience is beautiful, very quiet as it's location is far. If I have better option I will not visit here. Also the price is on the expensive side.

"Fantastic Coffee and Atmosphere!"

'Durbar Coffee and Eatery' is a must-visit! The coffee is rich and flavorful, and the staff are warm and welcoming. The cozy ambiance makes it perfect for relaxing or catching up with friends. My cappuccino was perfection, and their treats are equally delightful. Highly recommend!
Melbourne coffee scene in downtown Nepal! Great strong flat whites and even a magic?!? They played tame impala. It's basically Fitzroy
Best proper flat white (order the Magic) we’ve had in Nepal - and we’ve been to plenty of coffee shops. Barista used to work in Melbourne. Highly recommend.
Great place with great vibes, service and of course, great coffee!! ☕️🤎 thanks so much for the warm smiles and conversations

Finally a great coffee in Bhaktapur. Perfect flat white and latte. Barista knows his craft.
Friendly service in a quiet and interesting location, just off Dunbar square.
I've tried MANY cafes here, this is the standout.

address: M9GX+J7, Radhe Radhe Rd, Madhyapur Thimi, Nepal
review_text:
Darbar Thakali Grill – Extremely Disappointing Experience
Today, five of us went there after seeing the board claiming Thakali food, but the food was absolutely not Thakali in any sense. This was one of the worst dining experiences I have ever had.
The papad served on top of the food had hair on it, which is completely unacceptable and unhygienic. The gundruk pickle was already spoiled and had a foul smell. The cauliflower curry was overloaded with masala and extremely salty, while the green leafy vegetables were almost raw and tasteless. Even the black dal was poorly prepared and not good at all.
The chicken and mutton were very disappointing— not even as good as what you normally get at a roadside dhaba. Overall, the food quality, taste, and hygiene were shocking.
This is by far the worst Thakali food I have ever eaten, and I would not recommend this place to anyone.

address: M9GX+J7, Radhe Radhe Rd, Madhyapur Thimi, Nepal
review_text:
The ambiance is simple yet comfortable, with a clean dining space that reflects a traditional Nepali style. Service is generally quick and friendly, although it can get crowded during peak hours.
Overall, Durbar Thakali offers a satisfying and authentic dining experience. It’s a great place if you want to enjoy hearty, traditional Nepali food at a reasonable price.

address: M9GX+J7, Radhe Radhe Rd, Madhyapur Thimi, Nepal
review_text:
Worse experience I have in thakali kitchen ever in my life. We have to wait more than one hour to receive our ordered food. When thakali khana arrive there is only achar and dal and khana with Mustang Aalu. When we are adding up khana only khana after calling them 2 3 times for daal and tarkari we also need to call them for 2 3 times.
Live music in Thakali kitchen is not suitable not matching the vibes. It is really hard to talk with each other.
In summary the quality of Thakali khana is less than Staff khana. Quality of service is lower than the bhatti. It was waste of money and times as well.
Highly not recommend.

address: M9GX+J7, Radhe Radhe Rd, Madhyapur Thimi, Nepal
review_text:
The place and service at the Thakali restaurant were excellent — truly top-notch. However, the food itself was a big disappointment. The Thakali set was bland and far from authentic; it tasted more like a third-class version of what Thakali cuisine should be. Definitely not worth it for the food, though the ambiance and hospitality were impressive.

I am adding my review here 🤬

I had a terrible experience with the Thakali food at this restaurant. After eating there yesterday, my mother, my brother, and I all suffered from food poisoning. We were sick all night and couldn’t sleep properly. It seems like they might have served leftover or spoiled food. This is completely unacceptable and extremely disappointing. Be very careful if you’re planning to eat here.

address: M9GX+J7, Radhe Radhe Rd, Madhyapur Thimi, Nepal
review_text: Overall Experience was Good.  The whole chicken bbq grill was decent, timbur friend spicy noodles was best of all. The place is pretty spacious,  the ambience is quiet good.  We stayed at the top floor and it was quiet chilling. One can come with family and they serve pretty well specially the Thakali dishes. The staffs are pretty cooperative, moreover the service was fast. will be back there soon for Thakali platter.

Love their ambience, plenty of spaces with aesthetic decors and friendly staff!
• It's a perfect place for hanging out, chilling, taking photos.📸 I tried their pizza and blue angel, which was very good. Rating:
▪︎Pizza 9/10✨️
▪︎Blue angel 8.5/10. ✨️
•I think both the place and the food are impressive. Their staffs are very welcoming as well. It's a perfect place to dine in and enjoy the aesthetic of the cafe. It's very cozy with games and book. Overall nice place.
•Love to go back again to try more of their foods and welcoming aspects.

11/10!! Had such a great time at Cafe Boh! The place has this youthful, energetic vibe that instantly makes you feel relaxed and welcome. The service was excellent, friendly, attentive, and genuine. Now let’s talk about the drinks; absolutely amazing! I had three margaritas (couldn’t resist!) and one espresso martini, and each one was crafted perfectly. The flavors were well-balanced and refreshing, easily some of the best cocktails I’ve had in Kathmandu. We also ordered the Margarita pizza, and it was just as impressive, crispy, flavorful, and the perfect pairing with our drinks. Even though we were staying at Vivanta Hotel, which is a bit far from Cafe Boh, the trip was definitely worth it. The atmosphere, quality, and reasonable prices make this spot a must-visit for anyone looking to unwind and enjoy good food and great drinks in the city.

I recently visited Cafe Boh, and it is such a beautiful place with an amazing vibe ♥️
I actually discovered this café while strolling around Radhe Radhe. I was feeling tired at home that day and decided to go there last minute ,and I’m so glad I did!
The food is healthy and thoughtfully curated, perfect if you’re looking for something light yet satisfying. It’s such a lovely spot to enjoy coffee with your loved ones or catch up with friends.
The hospitality is truly awesome - warm, welcoming, and attentive. I loved it so much that I visited again today with my family, and the experience was just as wonderful. Lovely ambience ♥️
Definitely a place I’d recommend if you’re around Radhe Radhe .

Ambience was best and aesthetic.
We had Spaghetti Bacon Carbonara and Peri Peri Wings.

Carbonara was best but the peri peri was disappointing.

Overall it was good to be there.!
the bolognese sauce tasted more like momo fillings than bolognese.

address: Durbar square -2 Nearby Balakhu, Ganesh Temple, Bhaktapur 44500, Nepal
review_text: Epic experience - stayed there with our kids. Fantastic service, room and breakfast included! Picked us up from the bus, helped us get our tickets into Buktapur and took us to the best restaurant in Buktapur. Highly recommend!

address: Durbar square -2 Nearby Balakhu, Ganesh Temple, Bhaktapur 44500, Nepal
review_text: Great service. The manager (Jeevan) and other staff are very helpful and friendly. The breakfast and coffee especially are just amazing. The location is very close to all the tourist attractions in the Bakhtapur area. Overall, it was a great experience!

address: Durbar square -2 Nearby Balakhu, Ganesh Temple, Bhaktapur 44500, Nepal
review_text:
My stay at this hotel was truly optimal. The lobby, rooms, dining room and everywhere else was clean. The breakfast was delicious and filling. There was a ping pong table in the courtyard in front of the dining room, and the owner is a former ping pong player whose picture was on display. We played table tennis together and he was still very good.
I would definitely stay there again.

address: Durbar square -2 Nearby Balakhu, Ganesh Temple, Bhaktapur 44500, Nepal
review_text: Extremely clean and reasonable size room, nice and friendly boss, the served  breakfast is awesome, lobby and entrance is tastefully done.

address: Durbar square -2 Nearby Balakhu, Ganesh Temple, Bhaktapur 44500, Nepal
review_text: The manager named Jeevan was a super kind and nice. He kindly assisted us with our trips and hotel stay despite we were very demanding. He is open minded and generous to guests. The hotel is quite,  clean, and exotic and romantic. Location is just next to the plaza. Highly valuable for price.

address: MCCH+PQH Chatubrahma MahaVihar (Tadhichen Bahal), 12 Durbar Square Road, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: This place serves only baara and potatoes. The best you'll have. No fuss food place with the nicest ladies working there. Commonly known these days as Aama ko Baara. They don't have an official name. We're not used to the street food in Nepal since we moved to the US but we have never gotten sick eating there.

address: MCCH+PQH Chatubrahma MahaVihar (Tadhichen Bahal), 12 Durbar Square Road, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text:
Opens up around 1 or 2pm. Just bara (lentil pancakes) with the option to get it with an egg or ground meat cooked in. It's fresh and crisp and just great all around. Get the aloo achar (spicy potato pickle) as well. They might have a bean soup too, but they don't have chatamari (rice flour pancake), so not sure why someone put that in the name.

There wasn't a price list but it's pretty affordable. Three various baras and an aloo ko achar was 500 rupees.

address: MCCH+PQH Chatubrahma MahaVihar (Tadhichen Bahal), 12 Durbar Square Road, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: This place is famous for newari dish Baara and Chatamari. They have both veg and non veg baara. The taste is awesome. The place is a little conjusted but still many people come here for the awesome treat. Even Vmag has reviewed this place.

address: MCCH+PQH Chatubrahma MahaVihar (Tadhichen Bahal), 12 Durbar Square Road, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: This place was awesome. Food was freshly made and handed to you from the grill. Really unique dining experience with incredible food.

address: MCCH+PQH Chatubrahma MahaVihar (Tadhichen Bahal), 12 Durbar Square Road, Bhaktapur 44800, Nepal
review_text: They have been serving mouth-watering traditional Newari food called Woh (Baara in Nepali) and Chatamari. They serve authentic Newari taste at a very reasonable price. The people there are also very friendly and fun to talk to. You will love this place if you are a fan of Newari food.
